title: How The Ecolab (ECL) Story Is Shifting With CoolIT And Pricing Power Debates
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

The updated fair value estimate for Ecolab has edged to US$319.29, compared with the previous US$320.43, signaling a fine tuning rather than a reset of expectations. This shift sits against a backdrop of mixed analyst reactions, with some firms lifting targets on pricing confidence and others trimming them after the US$4.75b CoolIT acquisition and valuation questions. As you read on, you will see how these competing views shape the evolving narrative around Ecolab and what to watch next.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Ecolab.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Berenberg upgraded Ecolab to Buy from Hold with a US$326 target, pointing to planned 10% to 14% price increases across products, services and geographies, which it views as a lasting element of Ecolab's pricing power and a potential inflation benefit. Evercore ISI lifted its target to US$312 and maintained an Outperform view. Firms including Deutsche Bank, BMO, Seaport Research, BofA, UBS, Morgan Stanley, Mizuho, JPMorgan, Citi and Bradesco BBI also issued higher targets earlier in the year, signaling support for Ecolab's execution and earnings profile at higher valuation levels.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Wolfe Research highlighted what it sees as a hefty valuation attached to the US$4.75b CoolIT deal, indicating some concern about the price paid and the return on this capital allocation decision. Wells Fargo, Baird and UBS each reduced their Ecolab price targets more recently. This suggests that parts of the Street are reassessing upside as valuation, acquisition spending and execution risks are weighed more conservatively.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:ECL 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for Ecolab. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Ecolab is reported to be close to acquiring data center cooling specialist CoolIT from KKR in a transaction valued between US$4.5b and US$5b, signaling a large move into thermal management for digital infrastructure. Additional reporting also describes Ecolab as nearing a CoolIT deal in the US$4.5b to US$5b range, reinforcing expectations that the company is preparing for a sizeable acquisition in this area. The White House is preparing an E15 waiver for summer gasoline sales, which could influence fuel and ethanol markets that relate to parts of Ecolab’s customer base and cost structure.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Ecolab

Fair value estimate is now US$319.29, compared with US$320.43 previously. Assumed revenue growth rate is now 7.48%, compared with 7.20% previously. Projected net profit margin is now 15.13%, compared with 15.37% previously. Future P/E multiple is now 36.31x, compared with 36.22x previously. Discount rate is now 7.44%, compared with 7.50% previously.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company’s business story to a set of financial expectations and a fair value estimate so you can see what is priced in. They refresh as new data and research come through, so the view stays aligned with the latest information.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Ecolab to stay up to date on:

How the One Ecolab initiative, value based pricing and market share gains are expected to support revenue growth and higher net margins. The role of digital technology and Ecolab Digital subscription offerings in supporting operating income margins and earnings potential. Key risks around softer heavy industrial demand, higher tariff related and local supplier costs, and possible customer pushback on the 5% trade surcharge.

title: Will Ecolab (ECL) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
content:
Have you been searching for a stock that might be well-positioned to maintain its earnings-beat streak in its upcoming report? It is worth considering Ecolab (ECL), which belongs to the Zacks Chemical - Specialty industry.

When looking at the last two reports, this cleaning, food-safety and pest-control services company has recorded a strong streak of surpassing earnings estimates. The company has topped estimates by 0.73%, on average, in the last two quarters.

For the last reported quarter, Ecolab came out with earnings of $2.08 per share versus the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.06 per share, representing a surprise of 0.97%. For the previous quarter, the company was expected to post earnings of $2.06 per share and it actually produced earnings of $2.07 per share, delivering a surprise of 0.49%.

Price and EPS Surprise

For Ecolab, estimates have been trending higher, thanks in part to this earnings surprise history. And when you look at the stock's positive Zacks Earnings ESP (Expected Surprise Prediction), it's a great indicator of a future earnings beat, especially when combined with its solid Zacks Rank.

Our research shows that stocks with the combination of a positive Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) or better produce a positive surprise nearly 70% of the time. In other words, if you have 10 stocks with this combination, the number of stocks that beat the consensus estimate could be as high as seven.

The Zacks Earnings ESP compares the Most Accurate Estimate to the Zacks Consensus Estimate for the quarter; the Most Accurate Estimate is a version of the Zacks Consensus whose definition is related to change. The idea here is that analysts revising their estimates right before an earnings release have the latest information, which could potentially be more accurate than what they and others contributing to the consensus had predicted earlier.

Ecolab currently has an Earnings ESP of +0.06%, which suggests that analysts have recently become bullish on the company's earnings prospects. This positive Earnings ESP when combined with the stock's Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) indicates that another beat is possibly around the corner. We expect the company's next earnings report to be released on April 28, 2026.

Investors should note, however, that a negative Earnings ESP reading is not indicative of an earnings miss, but a negative value does reduce the predictive power of this metric.

Many companies end up beating the consensus EPS estimate, but that may not be the sole basis for their stocks moving higher. On the other hand, some stocks may hold their ground even if they end up missing the consensus estimate.

Story Continues

Because of this, it's really important to check a company's Earnings ESP ahead of its quarterly release to increase the odds of success. Make sure to utilize our Earnings ESP Filter to uncover the best stocks to buy or sell before they've reported.

title: Here's What to Expect From Ecolab's Next Earnings Report
content:
Ecolab Inc. (ECL), headquartered in Saint Paul, Minnesota, provides water, hygiene, and infection prevention solutions and services. With a market cap of $75.4 billion, the company's services include food safety, sanitation, optimization of water and energy use, improvement of operational efficiency and sustainability. The global leader in the cleaning and sanitation industry is expected to announce its fiscal first-quarter earnings for 2026 before the market opens on Tuesday, Apr. 28.

Ahead of the event, analysts expect ECL to report a profit of $1.70 per share on a diluted basis, up 13.3% from $1.50 per share in the year-ago quarter. The company beat or matched the consensus estimates in three of the last four quarters while missing the forecast on another occasion.

More News from Barchart

Nike’s Real Problem Isn’t Sales: It’s Relevance Why Are ORCL Stock Investors So Excited About Oracle Layoffs? Dear Costco Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for May 1 Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines.

For the full year, analysts expect ECL to report EPS of $8.46, up 12.4% from $7.53 in fiscal 2025. Its EPS is expected to rise 15.1% year over year to $9.74 in fiscal 2027.www.barchart.com

ECL stock has underperformed the S&P 500 Index’s ($SPX) 30.3% gains over the past 52 weeks, with shares up 12.3% during this period. Similarly, it underperformed the State Street Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF’s (XLB) 29% returns over the same time frame.www.barchart.com

On Feb. 10, ECL shares closed up by 4% after reporting its Q4 results. Its adjusted EPS of $2.08 exceeded Wall Street expectations of $2.06. The company’s revenue of $4.20 billion, topping Wall Street forecasts of $4.19 billion. ECL expects full-year adjusted EPS in the range of $8.43 to $8.63.

Analysts’ consensus opinion on ECL stock is reasonably bullish, with a “Moderate Buy” rating overall. Out of 28 analysts covering the stock, 15 advise a “Strong Buy” rating, two suggest a “Moderate Buy,” and 11 give a “Hold.” ECL’s average analyst price target is $322.10, indicating a potential upside of 20.6% from the current levels.

On the date of publication, Neha Panjwani did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. This article was originally published on Barchart.com
